---
title: "Project2"
format: html
editor: visual
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values
natural_gas_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Natural_Gas_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
crude_oil_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Energy_Type = c("Natural Gas", "Crude Oil"),
  Mean_Volume = c(natural_gas_mean, crude_oil_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Energy_Type, y = Mean_Volume, fill = Energy_Type)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Energy Type", y = "Mean Volume", title = "Mean Energy Volumes")

```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values
meta_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
google_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Google_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
apple_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Stock = c("Meta", "Google", "Apple"),
  Mean_Volume = c(meta_mean, google_mean, apple_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Stock, y = Mean_Volume, fill = Stock)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Stock", y = "Mean Volume", title = "Mean Trading Volume of Meta, Google, and Apple")
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values
meta_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
google_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Google_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
apple_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)
nasdaq_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Nasdaq_100_Vol., na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Stock = c("Meta", "Google", "Apple", "Nasdaq 100"),
  Mean_Volume = c(meta_mean, google_mean, apple_mean, nasdaq_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Stock, y = Mean_Volume, fill = Stock)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Stock", y = "Mean Volume", title = "Mean Trading Volume of Meta, Google, Apple, and Nasdaq 100")
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values for Bitcoin and Ethereum
bitcoin_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Bitcoin_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
ethereum_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Ethereum_Price, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Crypto = c("Bitcoin", "Ethereum"),
  Mean_Price = c(bitcoin_mean, ethereum_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Crypto, y = Mean_Price, fill = Crypto)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Cryptocurrency", y = "Mean Price", title = "Mean Price of Bitcoin and Ethereum")
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values for Bitcoin and Ethereum
bitcoin_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$`Bitcoin_Vol.`, na.rm = TRUE)
ethereum_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$`Ethereum_Vol.`, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Crypto2 = c("Bitcoin", "Ethereum"),
  Mean_vol = c(bitcoin_mean, ethereum_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Crypto2, y = Mean_vol, fill = Crypto2)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Cryptocurrency", y = "Mean volume", title = "Mean volume of Bitcoin and Ethereum")
```






```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean values for Natural Gas and Crude Oil
natural_gas_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Natural_Gas_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
crude_oil_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Crude_oil_Price, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a simple data frame for plotting
data <- data.frame(
  Energy = c("Natural Gas", "Crude Oil"),
  Mean_Price = c(natural_gas_mean, crude_oil_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Energy, y = Mean_Price, fill = Energy)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Energy", y = "Mean Price", title = "Mean Price of Natural Gas and Crude Oil")

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean prices
meta_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Meta_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
google_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Google_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
apple_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Apple_Price, na.rm = TRUE)

# Create a single data frame
data <- data.frame(
  Stock = c("Meta", "Google", "Apple"),
  Mean_Price = c(meta_mean, google_mean, apple_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Stock, y = Mean_Price, fill = Stock)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Stock", y = "Mean Price", title = "Mean Price of Meta, Google, and Apple") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate mean prices
nasdaq_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$Nasdaq_100_Price, na.rm = TRUE)
sp500_mean <- mean(Stock_Market_Dataset$`S&P_500_Price`, na.rm = TRUE)  # Use backticks for S&P 500

# Create a data frame
data <- data.frame(
  Index = c("Nasdaq 100", "S&P 500"),
  Mean_Price = c(nasdaq_mean, sp500_mean)
)

# Plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = Index, y = Mean_Price, fill = Index)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "Index", y = "Mean Price", title = "Mean Price of Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
#function to calculate percentage of missing values
percent_missing <- function(x) {
  mean(is.na(x)) * 100
}

# function to calculate percentage of outliers using IQR method
percent_outliers <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]  # Remove NAs
  q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  iqr <- q3 - q1
  lower <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
  upper <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  mean(x < lower | x > upper) * 100
}

# Variables to analyze
variables <- c("Natural_Gas_Price", "Natural_Gas_Vol.", "Meta_Price", "Meta_Vol.")

# Compute percentages
result <- data.frame(
  Variable = variables,
  Percent_Missing = sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset[ , variables], percent_missing),
  Percent_Outliers = sapply(Stock_Market_Dataset[ , variables], percent_outliers)
)
print(result)
```


```{r}
# Remove rows where Natural_Gas_Vol is NA
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset[!is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset$Natural_Gas_Vol), ]
# print missing vaules 
sum(is.na(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Vol))
```


```{r}
# Load ggplot2 for plotting
library(ggplot2)

# Create log-transformed plots
log_plot <- function(column_name, dataset) {
  # Create plot title
  plot_title <- paste("Log Transformation of", column_name)
  
  # Apply log 
  ggplot(dataset, aes_string(x = paste0("", column_name, ""))) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    labs(title = plot_title, x = paste("", column_name, "", sep = ""), y = "count") +
    theme_minimal()
}

# Plot Natural_Gas_Price
log_plot("Natural_Gas_Price", Stock_Market_Dataset2)

# Plot Natural_Gas_Vol
log_plot("Natural_Gas_Vol.", Stock_Market_Dataset2)

# Plot Meta_Vol
log_plot("Meta_Vol.", Stock_Market_Dataset2)
```


```{r}
# Load ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# Scatter plot between Natural_Gas_Price and Natural_Gas_Vol
ggplot(Stock_Market_Dataset2, aes(x = Natural_Gas_Price, y = Natural_Gas_Vol.)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Natural Gas Price vs Volume",
       x = "Natural Gas Price",
       y = "Natural Gas Volume") +
  theme_minimal()

```

















```{r}
# extracts Year and Month 
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Year <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%Y")
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Month <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%m")
Stock_Market_Dataset2$YearMonth <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%Y-%m")

# Yearly summary
natural_gas_yearly <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = mean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

# Monthly summary
natural_gas_monthly <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ YearMonth,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = mean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
print("Yearly Natural Gas Summary:")
print(natural_gas_yearly)

print("Monthly Natural Gas Summary:")
print(natural_gas_monthly)
```


```{r}
# Extract Year, Month, and YearMonth
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Year <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%Y")
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Month <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%m")
# YEARLY HIGH (max)
natural_gas_yearly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# YEARLY LOW 
natural_gas_yearly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# MONTHLY HIGH 
natural_gas_monthly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# MONTHLY LOW 
natural_gas_monthly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Natural_Gas_Price, Natural_Gas_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
#  print results
print(natural_gas_yearly_high)
print(natural_gas_yearly_low)
print(natural_gas_monthly_high)
print(natural_gas_monthly_low)
```


```{r}
# Extract Year, Month, and YearMonth
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Year <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%Y")
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Month <- format(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Date, "%m")
# YEARLY HIGH 
Meta_yearly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Meta_Price, Meta_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# YEARLY LOW 
Meta_yearly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Meta_Price, Meta_Vol.) ~ Year,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# MONTHLY HIGH 
Meta_monthly_high <- aggregate(
  cbind(Meta_Price, Meta_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = max,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
# MONTHLY LOW 
Meta_monthly_low <- aggregate(
  cbind(Meta_Price, Meta_Vol.) ~ Month,
  data = Stock_Market_Dataset2,
  FUN = min,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
#  print results
print(Meta_yearly_high)
print(Meta_yearly_low)
print(Meta_monthly_high)
print(Meta_monthly_low)
```










```{r}
# Correlation test for Bitcoin_Vol and Meta_Vol
vol_test <- cor.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Bitcoin_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol., use = "complete.obs")

# Correlation test for Bitcoin_Price and Meta_Price
price_test <- cor.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Bitcoin_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price, use = "complete.obs")

# Print results
print(vol_test)
print(price_test)
```




```{r}
# Load library
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
# Calculate returns for each asset
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
  mutate(
    Meta_Return = (Meta_Price / lag(Meta_Price)) - 1,
    Bitcoin_Return = (Bitcoin_Price / lag(Bitcoin_Price)) - 1,
    Google_Return = (Google_Price / lag(Google_Price)) - 1,
    Apple_Return = (Apple_Price / lag(Apple_Price)) - 1,
    Crude_oil_Return = (Crude_oil_Price / lag(Crude_oil_Price)) - 1,
    Natural_Gas_Return = (Natural_Gas_Price / lag(Natural_Gas_Price)) - 1,
    Ethereum_Return = (Ethereum_Price / lag(Ethereum_Price)) - 1,
    SNP500_Return = (`S&P_500_Price` / lag(`S&P_500_Price`)) - 1,
    Nasdaq_100_Return = (Nasdaq_100_Price / lag(Nasdaq_100_Price)) - 1
  )
#  Calculate volatility 
volatility_summary <- data.frame(
  Asset = c("Meta", "Bitcoin", "Google", "Apple", "Crude Oil", "Natural Gas", "Etherum", "S&P 500", "Nasdaq 100"),
  Volatility = c(
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Bitcoin_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Ethereum_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$SNP500_Return, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Nasdaq_100_Return, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)
# Sort by volatility
volatility_summary <- volatility_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(Volatility))

# View volatility table
print(volatility_summary)

# Plot volatility
ggplot(volatility_summary, aes(x = reorder(Asset, Volatility), y = Volatility, fill = Asset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Volatility of Different Assets (Standard Deviation of Returns)",
    x = "Asset",
    y = "Volatility"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```
```{r}
# Perform t-tests
t_test_meta_google <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Price)
t_test_meta_apple <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price)
t_test_google_apple <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Price)

t_test_meta_googlevol <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Vol.)
t_test_meta_applevol <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Vol.)
t_test_google_applevol <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Google_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Apple_Vol.)

# Display the results
t_test_meta_google
t_test_meta_apple
t_test_google_apple

t_test_meta_googlevol
t_test_meta_applevol
t_test_google_applevol
```


```{r}
# Perform t-tests 
t_test_bitcoin_ethereum <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Bitcoin_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Ethereum_Price)
t_test_bitcoin_ethereum_vol <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Bitcoin_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Ethereum_Vol.)

# Display the results
t_test_bitcoin_ethereum
t_test_bitcoin_ethereum_vol
```


```{r}
# Perform t-tests 
t_test_crude_gas <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Price, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price)
t_test_crude_gasvol <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Crude_oil_Vol., Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Vol.)

# Display the results
t_test_crude_gas
t_test_crude_gasvol
```
```{r}
# Perform t-tests 
t_test_sp_nasdaq <- t.test(Stock_Market_Dataset2$`S&P_500_Price`, Stock_Market_Dataset2$Nasdaq_100_Price)
# Display the results
t_test_sp_nasdaq

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)

# Assuming your data frame is named Stock_Market_Dataset2
# For illustration, we'll create a binary outcome variable based on median price

# Create binary outcome variable (1 if price > median, 0 otherwise)
Stock_Market_Dataset2$`S&P_500_Price_Binary` <- ifelse(Stock_Market_Dataset2$`S&P_500_Price` > median(Stock_Market_Dataset2$`S&P_500_Price`, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0)
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Nasdaq_100_Price_Binary <- ifelse(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Nasdaq_100_Price > median(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Nasdaq_100_Price, na.rm = TRUE), 1, 0)


# Split the dataset into training and testing sets (80% train, 20% test)
set.seed(42)  # For reproducibility
train_index <- createDataPartition(Stock_Market_Dataset2$`S&P_500_Price_Binary`, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[train_index, ]
test_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[-train_index, ]

# Fit a logistic regression model with S&P_500_Price and Nasdaq_100_Price as predictors
model <- glm(`S&P_500_Price_Binary` ~ `S&P_500_Price` + Nasdaq_100_Price, data = train_data, family = binomial)

# Summary of the model
summary(model)

# Make predictions on the test set
predictions <- predict(model, test_data, type = "response")
predicted_class <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Evaluate the model
confusion_matrix <- table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = test_data$`S&P_500_Price_Binary`)
print(confusion_matrix)

# Calculate accuracy
accuracy <- sum(diag(confusion_matrix)) / sum(confusion_matrix)
cat("Accuracy: ", accuracy, "\n")



```
```{r}
# Split data
set.seed(42)
train_index <- createDataPartition(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Meta_Price, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[train_index, ]
test_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[-train_index, ]

# Linear regression
model <- lm(Meta_Price ~ Apple_Price + Google_Price, data = train_data)
summary(model)

# Predict
predictions <- predict(model, test_data)

# RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error) for evaluation
rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data$Meta_Price)^2))
cat("RMSE:", rmse, "\n")


```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  mutate(Meta_Price_Tomorrow = lead(Meta_Price, 1))  # Shift prices to create tomorrow's price

# Now create a binary target variable for price movement
Stock_Market_Dataset2 <- Stock_Market_Dataset2 %>%
  mutate(Meta_Up = ifelse(Meta_Price_Tomorrow > Meta_Price, 1, 0))  # 1 if price went up, else 0

```
```{r}
# logistic regression
model <- glm(Meta_Up ~ Meta_Price + Google_Price + Apple_Price + Meta_Vol. + Google_Vol. + Apple_Vol., 
             family = "binomial", data = Stock_Market_Dataset2)

# Summary of the model
summary(model)


```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)


# Create the binary outcome variable (whether price goes up the next day)
Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price_Binary <- ifelse(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price > lag(Stock_Market_Dataset2$Natural_Gas_Price), 1, 0)

# Split the data into training and testing sets
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
train_index <- sample(1:nrow(Stock_Market_Dataset2), size = 0.7 * nrow(Stock_Market_Dataset2))
train_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[train_index, ]
test_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[-train_index, ]

# Fit the logistic regression model
model <- glm(Natural_Gas_Price_Binary ~ Natural_Gas_Price + Natural_Gas_Vol. + Crude_oil_Price + Crude_oil_Vol.,
             family = binomial, data = train_data)

# View model summary
summary(model)

# Make predictions on the test set
predictions <- predict(model, test_data, type = "response")

# Convert predictions to binary outcome (0 or 1)
predicted_class <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Evaluate accuracy
conf_matrix <- table(predicted_class, test_data$Natural_Gas_Price_Binary)
accuracy <- sum(diag(conf_matrix)) / sum(conf_matrix)
print(paste("Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4)))



```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Split into train and test sets
set.seed(123)
train_indices <- sample(1:nrow(Stock_Market_Dataset2), 0.7 * nrow(Stock_Market_Dataset2))
train_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[train_indices, ]
test_data <- Stock_Market_Dataset2[-train_indices, ]

# Train logistic regression model
model <- glm(Nasdaq_100_Price_Binary ~ `S&P_500_Price` + Nasdaq_100_Price + Nasdaq_100_Vol.,
             family = binomial, data = train_data)

summary(model)

# Predict on test set
predicted_probs <- predict(model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
predicted_classes <- ifelse(predicted_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Accuracy
conf_matrix <- table(Predicted = predicted_classes, Actual = test_data$Nasdaq_100_Price_Binary)
accuracy <- sum(diag(conf_matrix)) / sum(conf_matrix)
print(conf_matrix)
print(paste("Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4)))

```
